summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve*, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and the identification of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies collectively underscore the necessity of combining molecular data with morphological traits to clarify species boundaries within this globally distributed group, which inhabits diverse marine and estuarine environments.
